ThE CLANS
remains widespread among the Clans_ Classic religions exist tOgether with strange , hybrid creeds that have developed since the Exodus The doctrines of many; however Buddhism'$ pro hibition on taking llife or the Judeo-Christian-Muslim belief in an almighty God are incompatible with the martial: genetically engineered supremely self-confident Clan society  preventing their widespread acceptance. Clans Cloud Cobra, Coyote, Goliath Scorpion and Nova Cat have all developed their own forms of spirituality: The Cloud Cobras follow what they call The Way;' belief in God (or fate depending your point of view) in all torms The adnerents ot specific religions in this Clan gather in groups called Cloisters though these groupings have as much t0 do with politicae and persona Dorc as with religion The Coyotes_ Scorpions and Cats frequently use visions to guide them; The Coyote tradition derives from ancient Native American cultures_ vision quests and communing with nature central part of their lives Nova Cat beliefs are similar though their source is less identifiable; deprivation and hard ship are central both; stark contrast, the Goliath Scorpions addictive and often Iethal hallucinogens to alter their ceptions an attempt t0 rediscover the past. This practice is almost solely responsible for limiting the Scorpions' progress.
The Arts Most people of the Inner Sphere see Clan culture as drab utilitarian and military, bent solely on conquest: They might be surprised learn that the Clans have well developed artistic traditions The visual arts are widely practiced by Clansmen of every caste. warrior is as likely as merchant to be painter sculptor Certain cratts, however; are closely tied t0 specific castes For example , pottery most common among the arti- san subclass of the merchant caste. Much of Clan art extols the Clans past achievements_ particularly the victories of each Clan and the   mythology   surrounding their founders. The Kerenskys are favorite subject; as are great warriors Clan totems and landscapes
Media The Clans maintain tight control over print and broadcast media but no two Clans regard them in exactly the same way: At one extreme_ the Smoke Jaguars rarely allowed nonessen tial" broadcasts and So had virtually media industry: At the other; the Diamond Sharks pioneered the extensive Chatterweb information network_ and their well-developed media empire rivals many in the Inner Sphere The rest of the Clans lie some where in between Where the Inner Sphere broadcast media tend to be profit- driven Clan trideo tends toward indoctrination cven Morc 50 than the Capellan Confederation or the Draconis Combine , where intormation I5 also commonly manipulated withheld. Commercials are unknown_ benefit that many in more open Inner Sphere societies might appreciate
and the original Star League Like many things associated with the first Star League the Clans revere this language, to the Doint where contractions speech are socially proscribed un-Clanlike_ The Clans' tendency toward formality rcinforces this tradition; they consider the use contractions in speech and writing iIl-mannered and sloppy:
Religion Most Clansmen will say they are not religious but closer oor at Clan society offers different picture .
